section_name: Violations of Regulations or Prohibitions Imposed by Populous Counties on On-premise Signs
chapter_title: Regul. of Outdoor Signs on Rural Roads
text:
(a) If a county adopts a prohibition or regulation under Section 394.063 (On-premise Signs) , the attorney representing the county in district court may seek injunctive relief to prevent the violation or threatened violation of the prohibition or regulation.

(b) A violation of a prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.063 (On-premise Signs) is a Class C misdemeanor.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 394. Regul. of Outdoor Signs on Rural Roads
##### § 394.085 — Violations of Regulations or Prohibitions Imposed by Populous Counties on On-premise Signs

(a) If a county adopts a prohibition or regulation under Section 394.063 (On-premise Signs) , the attorney representing the county in district court may seek injunctive relief to prevent the violation or threatened violation of the prohibition or regulation.

(b) A violation of a prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.063 (On-premise Signs) is a Class C misdemeanor.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: If a county adopts a prohibition or regulation under Section 394.063 (On-premise Signs) , the attorney representing the county in district court may seek injunctive relief to prevent the violation or threatened violation of the prohibition or regulation.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A violation of a prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.063 (On-premise Signs) is a Class C misdemeanor.
